title: Dense Bitter Dutch Beef Stew
description: A hearty Dutch-inspired beef stew with bitter kale, warm cardamom, and a velvety finish whisked from cauliflower. Whole30-friendly and nostalgically comforting.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high. Brown beef cubes for 2–3 minutes per side, then remove and set aside.
Add onion, garlic, carrots, and turnip to the pot. Cook, stirring, for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in cauliflower florets and toast for 1–2 minutes.
Return browned beef to the pot. Pour in beef broth, add crushed cardamom pods and black pepper; stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 hour until beef is tender.
Add diced potatoes and kale; continue simmering, uncovered, for 20 minutes until vegetables are cooked.
Take a small portion of the cauliflower mixture, whisk it with a splash of broth to form a creamy slurry, then stir it back into the pot to thicken the stew.
Taste and adjust seasoning; optionally add a splash of red wine vinegar for brightness.
Remove from heat and serve hot. Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
